title: Scenario Foundry IR (kind: conversation)
description: A goal-bounded, multi-turn scenario compiled from mined intents + outcomes + satisfaction. Not a trace event type — this sits one layer above the JSONL trace format, compiled from emit.py --outcomes' intents.json + analysis.json. See docs/proposals/scenario-foundry.md#scenario-ir.

id.description: stable scenario id, e.g. scn-<session-slug>-<0000> for mined scenarios

source.description: distinguishes mined scenarios (compiled from a real corpus) from hand-authored gap-fillers; both are valid and coexist

provenance.description: traces back to the source session for audit/redaction review; always present on source=mined scenarios

session_id.description: the mined session id the scenario's first turn was compiled from

span_idx.description: the intent-span index (from instance_id '<session>#<span_idx>') the scenario starts on

persona.description: coarse actor label the scenario represents (e.g. core-maintainer, bugfix-contributor); mined scenarios derive this heuristically from action tags, hand-checked at calibration

goal.description: the user's goal for this scenario span, in their own words (verbatim first-turn text for mined scenarios)

expected_effects.description: deterministic, checkable outcomes the scenario should produce (derived from grounded mined actions, or hand-written for authored scenarios); may be empty when no action was groundable

abandoned.description: true when the mined span ends without a satisfying outcome (last turn was corrected with no further follow-up in the session) — such a scenario should assert the workbench does NOT silently claim success

role.description: mined scenarios only ever emit role=user turns today (verbatim user_text per intent span); assistant is reserved for future/hand-authored use

corrected.description: true when a later turn in the same session structurally or lexically corrected this turn's result (folded from analysis.json's satisfaction.corrected); a recall-biased review flag, not a verdict — see emit.py's satisfaction docstring

corrective_ops.description: grounded corrective git ops (e.g. 'git rebase --abort') found in the immediately-following span; present only when corrected is true and at least one op was grounded

followup_text_head.description: head (<=200 chars) of the user's own next words after this turn, lexically recovered; present only when corrected is true
